solution: x = x ^ y
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation: The Hamming distance between two integers can be calculated using the XOR operation between the two numbers. Since XOR operation has a constant time complexity, the solution has a high efficiency. The space complexity is also constant, making it a space-efficient solution. The energy implications are low as the solution only requires a few CPU operations.
